message.reasoning_content:
The user asks: "You are an expert molecular biologist. In a genome-wide CRISPRi Perturb-seq screen in mouse bone marrow-derived macrophages, predict the effect of knocking down {pert} on {gene}. Use only these labels: A=up-regulated, B=down-regulated, C=no significant effect. Most perturbation-gene pairs are C unless there is strong biological evidence. Return only one answer tag. No need to explain."

We need to fill in {pert} and {gene}. The user didn't specify actual perturbation or gene; they used placeholders {pert} and {gene}. So we have no specific info. According to instruction, we should respond with a single answer tag. Since we have no evidence, default to C (no significant effect). So output <answer>C</answer>.
